channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Bangladesh seeks extradition of ousted leader Sheikh Hasina from India
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Bangladesh has sent a formal request to India to extradite ousted Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina, who fled to New Delhi in August amid the student-led protests that ended her 15 years in power, the country’s foreign affairs adviser said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUR RAHMAN JAHANGIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump could face renewed ISIS threat in Syria as Turkey goes after US ally
item.description: Concerns are mounting that President-elect Trump could face an ISIS resurgence in Syria as Turkey-backed forces hit U.S.-allied SDF troops in a move that could divide the coalitions abilities to fight ISIS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France has a new government. Now it must fix the budget while avoiding collapse
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron’s office announced a new government Monday, after the previous Cabinet collapsed in a historic vote prompted by fighting over the country's budget.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former Greek royal family expresses 'deep emotion' after regaining citizenship
item.description: Members of Greece’s former royal family have expressed “deep emotion” after being granted Greek citizenship, ending a decades-old dispute with the country's government.
item.dc:creator: Derek Gatopoulos, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Matt Petgrave, involved in play that led to Adam Johnson's death, asks for donations for legal costs
item.description: Hockey player Matt Petgrave has called the death of Adam Johnson “a tragic accident” in a public appeal for donations to his legal costs amid a police investigation.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Magdeburg mourns Christmas market attack victims as fears swirl of deeper German social divisions
item.description: Mourners are laying flowers near the scene of the deadly Christmas market attack as investigators puzzle over the motive of the suspect and his previous encounters with authorities are scrutinized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia approves extradition of former US Marine over alleged training of Chinese military pilots
item.description: NEWCASTLE, Australia (AP) — Former U.S. Marine Corps pilot Daniel Duggan will be extradited from Australia to the United States over allegations that he illegally trained Chinese aviators.
item.dc:creator: BY KEIRAN SMITH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Many Americans have come to rely on Chinese-made drones. Now lawmakers want to ban them
item.description: The economic and technological rivalry between the U.S. and China has come to the drone market, where Chinese-made flying devices are a dominant player in America.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bethlehem plans another somber Christmas under the shadow of war in Gaza
item.description: BETHLEHEM, West Bank (AP) — The Nativity Store in Manger Square has sold handmade olive wood carvings and religious items to people visiting the traditional birthplace of Jesus since 1927.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: U.S. military sales and assistance aim to help Taiwan defend itself and deter China from launching an attack.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Stampedes in Nigeria leave at least 32 dead during Christmas food distribution
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — The death toll from stampedes during two Christmas charity events in Nigeria has increased from 13 to 32, police said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DYEPKAZAH SHIBAYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to track Santa's flight around the world this year
item.description: When Saint Nick starts his journey on Dec. 24, families can track Santa's sleigh using the NORAD Santa tracker.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope to skip outdoor Sunday prayer after catching cold days ahead of Christmas Eve, Day Masses
item.description: Pope Francis is suffering from a cold and will give the Sunday blessing ahead of a busy week that includes Christmas Eve and Day Masses and the start of the Holy Year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden offers condolences, cooperation with Germany after Christmas market attack
item.description: President Biden issued his condolences on Saturday over an attack on a Christmas market in Germany that left five dead and injured 200. “No community—and no family—should have to endure such a despicable and dark event, especially just days before a holiday of joy and peace,” Biden wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, The Hill

item.title: Controversy plagued UN agency that employed Oct. 7 terrorists facing new problems as country redirects funding
item.description: UN agencies are reportedly offering themselves as replacements to scandal hit UNRWA that's been riddled with accusations of terrorism since the Hamas terror massacre in southern Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 2 dead and 60 hurt after a car drives into a German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities called a deliberate attack.
item.dc:creator: EBRAHIM NOROOZI, CHRIS STERN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors Without Borders points to 'clear signs of ethnic cleansing' by Israel in Gaza
item.description: Doctors Without Borders said Israel is committing “clear signs of ethnic cleansing” in its war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Biden missing in action as Turkey inches closer to full-blown war against US-allied Kurds in Syria
item.description: Amid the end of the nearly 14 years of civil war in Syria, Turkish strongman Recep Tayyip Erdoğan is set to rekindle a new war in northern Syria against America’s main ally, the Syrian Kurds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 dead, many injured after man drives into German Christmas market in suspected terror attack: report
item.description: A festive Christmas market in Madgeburg, Germany erupted into chaos after a car was driven into a group of people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Malaysia resuming search for MH370 wreckage
item.description: Malaysian officials approved a third probe into the Indian Ocean with the goal of recovering wreckage from flight MH370, which disappeared more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US officials travel to Syria in search of missing Americans, meet with rebel leaders
item.description: Top U.S. officials are in Syria making the first face-to-face contact with rebel groups that took over the country in a lightning offensive earlier this month, their mission aimed at searching for kidnapped American journalist Austin Tice and other missing Americans.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Palestinian Authority under pressure amid rising resistance, popularity of Iran-backed terror groups
item.description: Palestinian Authority’s crackdown on terrorists intensifies, but efforts to regain control of the city and surrounding areas face fierce resistance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China skirts US efforts to stiff-arm CCP interference by bolstering state, local relations
item.description: China is skirting U.S. efforts to block its interference in areas ranging from trade to national security by cozying up with state officials to foster relations and keep its influence active in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says it has detained a suspect in the Moscow bombing that killed a senior general
item.description: Russia's security service said Wednesday it detained a citizen from Uzbekistan in the bombing that killed a senior general as he left his southeastern Moscow apartment — a bold assassination that was claimed by Ukraine's security service. Lt. Gen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu says Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that Israeli forces will stay in a buffer zone on the Syrian border, seized after the ouster of Syria's President Bashar Assad, until another arrangement is in place “that ensures Israel's security.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
